candidates.rationale: Oxidation via chemical pre-treatment converts As(III) to As(V) for better removal. Iron-based adsorptive media then removes arsenic through surface complexation. Chlorine provides final disinfection for drinking water safety.
Aeration oxidizes arsenic species for better removal. Ion exchange removes arsenic using specialty resins. UV provides final disinfection without chemical addition.
Permanganate oxidizes arsenic for improved removal. GAC removes arsenic through adsorption while also removing oxidation byproducts. Chloramine provides lasting residual disinfection.
Chemical oxidation followed by conventional treatment with coagulation/flocculation removes arsenic through precipitation and settling. Ozone provides powerful final disinfection.

Hydrogen peroxide oxidizes arsenic species. Direct filtration with coagulation removes arsenic through precipitation and filtration. Chlorine dioxide provides final disinfection with minimal byproduct formation.
